title: poupartia axillaris
intro: Poupartia axillaris, also known as the poupartia tree or stinkwood, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. The plant's bark contains a compound called poupartin, which exhibits medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Poupartia axillaris has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. The plant's herbal preparations include a decoction of the bark, which can be used to make teas or infusions, as well as a tincture that can be used in traditional medicine.
